text: Protein supplementation during chronic endurance training increases aerobic capacity (VO2peak) by approximately 0.89 mL/kg/min compared to training without supplementation.
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training

text: Protein supplementation during endurance training stimulates greater lean mass gain of approximately 0.32 kg compared to non-supplemented controls.
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training

text: Protein supplementation during endurance training improves time trial performance by approximately 29 seconds compared to non-supplemented controls.
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training

text: Protein supplementation provides an additional 26.4% gain in VO2peak on top of the improvements already achieved through endurance training alone.
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training

text: Endurance training alone substantially improves VO2peak by approximately 3.67 mL/kg/min, and protein supplementation augments this adaptation further.
specific_value: 3.67 mL/kg/min (95% CI: 2.32-5.03) from training; additional ~0.97 from protein
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training

text: The combined effect of protein supplementation on aerobic capacity measures (VO2peak and peak workload power) yields a small-to-moderate standardized effect size of 0.36 during endurance training.
population: healthy and clinical populations performing endurance training
